concepts.name: Boundary contract
concepts.definition: The jit-agents boundary is a function composition: spec name + invocation context → compiled spec → execution (mode-selected) → typed result OR final-assistant-message. Consumers interact through four surfaces: (1) Loading — loadAgent(name, loaderContext) → AgentSpec resolves the spec, fully resolves all relative paths, returns self-contained spec. (2) Compilation — compileAgent(spec, invocationContext) → CompiledAgent renders templates, produces final prompt, resolves contextBlocks. (3) Execution — executeAgent(compiled, dispatchContext) → TypedResult | FinalAssistantMessage dispatches per the mode selected from spec shape (DEC-0009); validates output (classifier-mode) or returns final message after multi-turn loop (agentic-mode). (4) Introspection — agentContract(name) → AgentContract exposes spec contract without executing. Key invariant: once an AgentSpec leaves the boundary, every path it references is absolute.

concepts.name: Agent signature
concepts.definition: The canonical signature reflects the full agent contract including capability-granted tool execution: agent :: (input matching inputSchema, context matching contextBlocks, declared tools[] validated as a subset of dispatch.parentGrant per DEC-0011) → output matching outputSchema (classifier-mode) OR final-assistant-message after multi-turn tool-execution loop completion (agentic-mode). The .agent.yaml spec declares: input contract (inputSchema validated pre-spawn); context parameterization (contextBlocks block names injected into template environment); capability declaration (tools[] operations the agent intends to invoke; non-subset grants throw GrantViolationError before LLM dispatch per DEC-0011); output contract (output.format and output.schema); prompt composition (system and task templates rendered with Nunjucks); model + thinking (resolved against ExtensionContext per DEC-0001; thinking enforcement per DEC-0002).

concepts.name: Dispatch shape
concepts.definition: executeAgent supports two distinct execution shapes selected per spec shape (DEC-0009). Classifier-mode dispatch shape: single LLM call; when outputSchema set forced phantom-tool toolChoice constrains output to structured payload; when outputSchema absent free-form text output with no phantom tool. Agentic-mode dispatch shape: multi-turn dispatch loop; declared tools[] must be a subset of dispatch.parentGrant per DEC-0011 or executeAgent throws GrantViolationError before LLM dispatch; the allowed compiled ToolDefinitions become the LLM's tool registry; LLM emits response; if tool calls are present executeAgent invokes each matching compiled ToolDefinition.execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) per DEC-0008/DEC-0010, passing ExtensionContext as ctx, threads results into message history, and calls LLM again with enriched history; loop terminates on end_turn or max-turns or tool-failure. Phantom-tool not used in agentic-mode.

concepts.name: What jit-agents owns
concepts.definition: Agent spec parsing (YAML → typed AgentSpec). Spec resolution (tier-aware discovery; relative-to-absolute path resolution). Template compilation (Nunjucks env construction; prompt rendering; shared macro library; contextBlocks injection with framework-level anti-injection wrapping). Prompt building (system + task + output instructions). Mode selection (per DEC-0009 algorithm). Classifier-mode in-process dispatch (existing: phantom-tool forced toolChoice; single LLM call; output extraction + validation against outputSchema). Agentic-mode in-process dispatch (pending implementation: multi-tool dispatch loop wiring compiled.tools to LLM tool registry after DEC-0011 grant enforcement; iterate tool-call responses; invoke matching compiled ToolDefinition.execute(...), passing ExtensionContext as ctx; thread results; loop termination). Capability-grant enforcement (DEC-0011 reject-on-non-subset at executeAgent boundary; granted capabilities exercised at agentic-mode dispatch not discarded post-check). Phantom tool construction (classifier-mode). Output validation. Agent contract introspection. Model resolution (DEC-0001 ExtensionContext lookup; FGAP-115 interlock).

concepts.name: What jit-agents does not own
concepts.definition: Subprocess dispatch (pi --mode json) — stays in pi-workflows (workflow-specific orchestration; DEC-0005). Workflow orchestration — DAG planning, step execution, state persistence, checkpoint/resume — owned by pi-workflows. Monitor orchestration — classification event routing, steering, activation, pattern learning — owned by pi-behavior-monitors. Project block CRUD — owned by pi-context; jit-agents consumes pi-context's readBlock API for contextBlocks injection but does not own substrate read/write. Domain specs — workflow YAMLs and monitor JSONs stay in their consumer packages. Tool implementations — jit-agents is tool-agnostic per DEC-0010; tools are not directly imported into jit-agents source; resolved at runtime via ExtensionContext.
